channel.title: Posts on The Self-Hosting Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on The Self-Hosting Blog

item.title: 📰Issue #21 • We're back
item.description: It's been a while! Hope that you had an awesome holiday period and new year 🎉 Apologies for our lack of newsletters! I'm afraid that we've been quite busy with other commitments.

item.title: Issue #20 • Competition time & Fun with Ansible!
item.description: Happy Friday! What a crazy month it's been as well, we've been looking into a lot of ansible stuff these past few weeks in preparation for some new features coming to the blog, so watch this space!! W

item.title: Issue #19 • Production Wordpress migrations
item.description: Previously running in dedicated Wordpress hosting, now running inside Docker containers. I'll probably do a write-up just to share my experience of fighting with DNS, Certificates, Duplication Tools,

item.title: Issue #18 • Blog expansion over to Forem
item.description: 📰 The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #18 What's new? We've been playing around with Forem [https://www.forem.com/] the backend that powers dev.to [https://dev.to]. We've been thinking about bringing the co

item.title: Self hosting Stringer, the anti-social RSS reader
item.description: if you're like me, I just want to consume my RSS. I don't want to see any adverts, any fancy recommendations or have a subscription fee. I also want an RSS reader that is pleasant to use. I have dabbl

item.title: Self hosting your own Matrix server on a Raspberry Pi (Updated 2022)
item.description: How to Self Host Matrix, an open source project that publishes the Matrix open standard for secure, decentralised, real-time communication.

item.title: Issue #17 • New Horizons
item.description: Happy Monday! Things have been quiet here, both of us have had far too many distractions this past month! Having said that, here is our first monthly dose of all things self hosting!

item.title: Issue #16 • We're moving to monthly
item.description: 📰 The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #16 Happy Monday! I have some news, I don't feel like I am producing quality when I write-up the Weekly Newsletter. Therefore, James and I have decided to run the Newsl

item.title: Issue #15 • Tell me when I suck
item.description: 📰 The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #15 Happy Monday! Here's your weekly dose of self-hosting goodies! Thanks! Ed -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 🎯 Top pos

item.title: Issue #14 • Are your services running?
item.description: 📰 The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #14 Happy Monday! Here's your weekly dose of self-hosting goodies! Have you ever thought about being notified when or if your services go down? Shukuri Adams [https://g

item.title: Issue #13 • Switching to iPhone from Android
item.description: 📰 The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #13 Happy Monday! Here's your weekly dose of self-hosting goodies! It's been quite a slow week in the self-hosting world, so I don't have that much for you. However, ha

item.title: Issue #12 • Save time by using the Raspberry Pi Imager's hidden feature
item.description: Forget needing to add your ssh and wpa_supplicant files! Here's a way to do it from the Raspberry Pi Imager

item.title: Issue #11 • Free tunnels from Cloudflare
item.description: 📰 The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #11 Happy Monday! Here's your weekly dose of self-hosting goodies! -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 🎯 Top posts from our

item.title: Issue #10 • IP Masquerading and server notifications
item.description: The top post from Inlets raises an interesting way of having a public static IP address without needing to pay your ISP for the pleasure. This can also be achieved for free using IP Masquerading, whic

item.title: Issue #9 • Obfuscate your online identity with an email alias
item.description: In this weeks' issue, I'm going to talk a little about setting up SimpleLogin, we'll be doing a full tutorial post in the future but this is more to make you aware of the service and how it can protec

item.title: Issue #8 • De-Google! Beat the YouTube algorithm, follow your subscriptions with RSS.
item.description: You may have noticed, while viewing your favorite YouTube content creators, that they ask you to "subscribe and click the bell icon" to be notified when they upload.

item.title: Issue #7 • Don't underestimate the power of a Raspberry Pi 4
item.description: The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #7 Happy Monday! Hope you had a good weekend! This week, we learned that we should never underestimate the power of a Raspberry Pi 4 [https://amzn.to/3cWTlno]. u/Swamp7hi

item.title: Self-hosting Papercups.io - Open-source live customer chat with Slack integration!
item.description: Papercups is an opensource self-hosted chat window that you can add to your site to interact with your visitors instantly. Papercups gives you the ability to reply directly from Slack or using their b

item.title: Issue #6 • The self-hosted alternative to Trello and Notion
item.description: The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #6 Happy Monday! Hope you had a good weekend! Focalboard [https://focalboard.com] has taken the internet by storm this week, introducing itself as an open source, self-ho

item.title: Setting up Focalboard - A self-hosted alternative to Trello and Notion
item.description: Focalboard is a self-hosted alternative to tools such as Notion or Trello which you can run on your own instance. Focalboard helps with tracking and organising your own work, as well as across a team.

item.title: Host your static blog for free with the Digital Ocean App Platform
item.description: The Digital Ocean App Platform allows you to host 3 static sites for free. Featuring a Cloudflare CDN, Hugo Static site generator, Github build pipelines, automatic HTTPS, custom domain name and DDoS

item.title: Issue #5 • Secure, decentralized communication
item.description: The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #5 Happy Monday! Hope you had a good weekend! We've joined Matrix [https://matrix.org/]! An open network for secure, decentralized communication. Join our official Self-H

item.title: Completely Self-Hosting Plausible.io. A privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics
item.description: Plausible is a lightweight, ethical, open-source and privacy-friendly Google Analytics alternative. We're going to show you how to self-host it

item.title: Issue #4 • Modern usability of the web
item.description: The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #4 Happy Monday! Hope you had a good weekend. We've had a very busy week so we've got tons of updates to share with you! We came across https://grumpy.website/ this week,

item.title: Setting up a Kubernetes cluster (K3s) using Raspberry Pi's and Portainer
item.description: Enter Kubernetes! an open-source platform for container management. I've been experimenting with this and a cluster of Raspberry Pi's for the past week and wanted to share what I've discovered thus fa

item.title: We don't care who you are
item.description: At The Self-Hosting Blog, we are huge advocates for online privacy. We believe that you should never have to compromise your own privacy, just to use the internet. This is why we've decided that we ca

item.title: Issue #3 • We should be back to normal soon
item.description: The Self-Hosting Blog • Issue #3 Ed here at The Self-Hosting Blog. Happy Monday! Hope you had a good weekend. If you're in the UK, we've had some good news that everything should be back to normal by

item.title: Setting up Portainer. Docker Container management made easy
item.description: Portainer is a lightweight management UI that allows you to easily manage your Docker host or Kubernetes Clusters. Portainer is as simple to deploy as it is to use.

item.title: Setting up a Valheim server using Docker
item.description: I'm going to talk through how to host a Valheim server on a docker image. This will enable you to set up a permanent server for all your friends to enjoy, 24/7.

item.title: Issue #2 • RIP LastPass...
item.description: The Self-Hosting Blog Ed here at The Self-Hosting Blog. I hope you're having a great week! This week, LastPass announced that they are feature-locking their free tier. Forcing users to choose whether

item.title: Issue #1 • Self-Hosting, why?
item.description: The Self-Hosting Blog Ed here at The Self-Hosting Blog. I hope you're having a great week! Thanks so much for choosing to subscribe to our mailing list. I really hope we can provide you with some usef

item.title: Why should you should spend the time, effort (and money) on self-hosting?
item.description: Self-hosting. Why? What's the point? Everything you could possibly use is already available online. Why should you spend the time and effort (and money) self-hosting projects. A question that I have a

item.title: Don't be alarmed!
item.description: I started this blog around 6 months ago as a place to share problems that I encountered whilst setting up my server in the loft. I often found, on my quest for digital freedom, problems that were rath

item.title: How to self-host PiHole, an internet advertising black hole
item.description: This article will cover setting up your own self-hosted PiHole instance. Allowing you to run an internal DNS and block internet advertisements from reaching your machine.

item.title: 10 of the BEST Self-hosting Subreddits To Follow (In 2021)
item.description: I've been using Reddit for many years now, and I have curated a list of Subreddits that I believe cover all the countless issues I have encountered during my journey of self-hosting.

item.title: Archiving ProtonMail Emails on a headless Ubuntu instance
item.description: I wanted to be able to store all of my ProtonMail emails locally for archival purposes, with the intention of running a local content search whenever I needed something retrieving.

item.title: Schiit Modi 2 - Fixing Windows not recognizing USB device
item.description: Schiit Modi 2 - Fixing Windows not recognizing USB device

item.title: Self-hosting a Wireguard VPN, the easy way
item.description: We're going to cover setting up a Wireguard VPN on your home server or cloud service. For secure remote access to your internal network, or a cheap, secure connection to a cloud service for some incre

item.title: Completely Self-Hosting Bitwarden Password Manager (Updated 2022)
item.description: This article will cover setting up your own self-hosted Bitwarden instance with Docker and configuring ngnix to allow for public exposure for cross device access to your vault.

item.title: How to completely self-host Standard Notes (Updated 2021)
item.description: This is a tutorial on how to self-host Standard Notes, for security, privacy and control of data

item.title: How to Set Up a Headless Syncthing Network
item.description: This is a tutorial on how to setup Syncthing for encrypted P2P file transfers and synchronisation

item.title: Configuring Vim for Node Js Development
item.description: An overview of the ideal vim plugins to use for Node Js development, with config file examples

item.title: My Self Hosted Note Syncing Journey Once Switching to Iphone
item.description: Recently moving to the iPhone meant that I had to re-think and re-design my existing note-taking solution, this is a quick writeup of my journey.

item.title: How to Route Nginx Through a Child Nginx Configuration - Jumpbox to Web Server Configuration
item.description: Routing nginx through another nginx instance from a central jumpbox server

item.title: Setting Up UFW on Ubuntu Server
item.description: UFW is a program that allows you to internally control ports on your Linux instance. This gives you the ability to forward ports from your machine.
